prompt: The UI freezes for 5 seconds when the button is clicked. What is the bug?
choices: Thread.sleep() is not available inside ActionListeners
The ActionListener runs on the EDT — blocking it with Thread.sleep() prevents the EDT from processing repaints and other events, freezing the UI
btn.setText() cannot be called from inside the ActionListener that belongs to btn
The JFrame must use invokeLater() for ActionListeners to work correctly
explanation: ActionListeners run on the Event Dispatch Thread. The EDT is responsible for all UI painting and event handling. Blocking it (with sleep, network I/O, or heavy computation) freezes the entire UI until the block completes. Fix: perform slow work on a background thread using SwingWorker or a separate Thread, then call setText() back on the EDT via SwingUtilities.invokeLater().
